sanskrit:
यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम्।
यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः॥
transliteration:
yatanto yoginaś cainaṃ paśyanty ātmany avasthitam |
yatanto 'py akṛtātmāno nainaṃ paśyanty acetasaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Endeavoring yogis see this (Self) seated in the heart; those who have not integrated the self, though striving, do not see it—unawake minds.
contextual_meaning: Only when attention is refined through sustained, integrated practice does the organizing center of personality (Self) become perceptible; mere effort without inner integration leaves one blind to the core of identity.
psychological_state: Post-effort reflection—recognition that disciplined practice, not just willpower, is required for self-insight; frustration at invisible progress.
trigger_scenario: After months of meditation apps and self-help books you still feel stuck; a mentor points out that collecting techniques without inner integration keeps the ‘self’ hidden.
anchor_text: Still your mind through steady practice to see your true self within.
